title: Math 314 Final Practice
description: Linear algebra with full context and formula-focused answers.

questions.prompt: Given vectors $$u=\begin{bmatrix}1\-2\1\end{bmatrix}, v=\begin{bmatrix}0\1\2\end{bmatrix}, w=\begin{bmatrix}-5\-2\1\end{bmatrix}$$. Orthogonality uses $$u\cdot v = 0$$. Are u,v,w orthogonal?
Independence rule: orthogonal nonzero vectors ⇒ independent. Given u,v,w above, determine independence.
questions.explanation: All vectors are nonzero and orthogonal.

questions.prompt: Span rule: $$span = independent\;vectors\;in\;R^3$$. For 3 independent vectors in R^3, do they span R^3?
questions.explanation: Dimension matches number of independent vectors.

questions.prompt: Basis rule: $$basis = independent + span$$. For u,v,w in R^3, verify basis.
questions.explanation: They are independent and span R^3.

questions.prompt: Given matrix $$A=\begin{bmatrix}1&0&-5\-2&1&0\1&2&5\end{bmatrix}$$. Pivot rule: $$independent = pivots = columns$$. Row reduce to check pivots.
Rank-nullity formula: $$rank = total\;columns - nullity$$. For a 7x6 matrix with nullity 4.
Dimension formula: $$dim(W^\perp) = n - dim(W)$$. If W ⊂ R^5 has dim 2.
Dependence rule: $$dependent = vectors > dimension$$. For 4 vectors in R^3.
Injective rule: $$injective = null(A) = 0$$. What defines injectivity?
Diagonalization rule: $$diagonalizable = n\;independent\;eigenvectors$$ for nxn matrix.
Invertibility rule: $$invertible = pivots = n$$. For nxn matrix.
Diagonalization inverse: $$A^{-1} = P D^{-1} P^{-1}$$. Identify method.
Eigenvalue power rule: $$A^2 = PDP^{-1} PDP^{-1}$$. If eigenvalues are ±1.
Determinant formula: $$det(A) = product\;of\;eigenvalues$$.
Vector space rule: $$Ax=0 ⇒ subspace$$.
Rank-nullity again: $$rank + nullity = n$$ for 3x3 matrix with rank 2.
Transpose null space: $$dim(null(A^T)) = m - rank(A)$$.
Diagonalization issue: $$repeated\;eigenvalues ⇒ may\;lack\;eigenvectors$$.
Guarantee rule: $$distinct\;eigenvalues ⇒ independent\;eigenvectors$$.
Rotation matrix eigenvalues: $$λ = cosθ ± i sinθ$$. When real?
Symmetric definition: $$A = A^T$$.
